verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone retained by a C-terminal KDEL sequence. However, it is robustly documented on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under multiple non-baseline conditions: (1) cancer cells broadly display GRP78 on the cell surface (cancer ecto-chaperone), making it a validated oncology target with multiple surface-directed antibody, ADC, and CAR-T programs in clinical/preclinical development; (2) cell-death/stress states (ER stress, hypoxia, nutrient deprivation) drive surface translocation; (3) apoptotic/ICD contexts also expose it. The surface pool is wash-resistant in cancer cells per surface biotinylation and flow cytometry data. This is a well-characterized cell_state_induced surface display — specifically the cancer-cell ecto-chaperone phenomenon. Not tissue_restricted in the narrow germline sense; rather oncogenic-state-driven surface display across many tumor types.
